place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast area and wanted to share my thoughts on East Coast Park. It remains one of the best-maintained spaces in Singapore, and its extensive connectivity to other parks makes it an ideal destination for long walks or cycling trips. I particularly enjoyed the ability to ride my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay via the park connector; it was always such a calm and refreshing experience.

The park offers a wonderful atmosphere with its blend of lush greenery and sea views. It is exceptionally well-equipped for families and fitness enthusiasts, featuring dedicated exercise stations, children’s play areas, and wide, separate paths for cycling and walking that prevent the space from feeling crowded.

Beyond the scenery, the facilities are excellent. There are convenient bicycle rentals, a variety of dining options in the restaurant area where you can enjoy the sea breeze, and well-maintained BBQ pits and shelters. For those interested in staying longer, the designated camping areas are a great touch, and I’ve always found the washrooms and bathing facilities to be very clean.

Overall, it is a perfect place to relax, exercise, and enjoy nature, whether you are visiting alone or with friends and family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast Park is one of the best and most well-maintained parks in Singapore. It is a very big park and is beautifully connected to other parks across Singapore, making it perfect for long walks or cycling trips. The park has lots of nice scenery, with greenery and sea views that make the atmosphere very relaxing. In some areas, there are exercise equipment stations and children’s play areas, which are great for families and fitness lovers.

There is a dedicated cycling path and a separate walking path, both of which are wide and well maintained, so it never feels crowded. You can also rent bicycles, and there are fun activities and attractions that bring in many people. The park has a restaurant area where you can enjoy a variety of foods while feeling the cool sea breeze. You can book BBQ pits, and there are huts available for shelter in case of rain. There are also designated camping areas for those who want to stay overnight.

I used to live near the East Coast area, and what I loved most was riding my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay, as the park connector makes this possible. It was always a calm and refreshing experience. On top of that, the washrooms and bathing areas are very clean and well maintained. Overall, East Coast Park is a perfect place to relax, exercise, eat, and enjoy nature, whether alone, with friends, or with family.

review_text: Nice place to walk, enjoy the sea breeze. Or one can enjoy coffee/drinks at one of the restaurant or cafe or even hawker centre. Cycling or inline blading can let u exercise and head to less crowded spot on the further end of the beach.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This is one of the most beautiful parks I’ve ever visited in Singapore.

It has plenty of trees, clean walking paths, and comfortable benches where people can relax and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. I especially recommend visiting in the morning because the weather is cooler and the park feels very refreshing

There are also many trash bins around the park, so it is convenient for visitors who bring drinks or snacks. The area is well maintained, clean, and perfect for taking a walk, reading a book, or simply spending quiet time away from the busy city. 🌳

review_text: This place is very convenient! It has a mall, a MRT station, and a bus interchange, all at the same place!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
One of the older train stations, one of the first air-con bus interchanges. Central area with many facilities like HDB (not sure if it's still there? Some areas are under renovation, atm, shops, eateries, supermarket, banks, etc.

Now it's probably less popular because most MRTs has modern-styled mall now, while Toa Pa Yoh is still the open air shopping area with small shops that come with all the mess, crowds under the mercy of Singapore's humid weather.

It's still nostalgic to visit, with its prominent yellow colors. All the shops and facilities mentioned above are just some walking distance away, including various buses to go anywhere in the country.

review_text:
If you’re curious about what shopping in Singapore might have felt like before all the shiny, towering megamalls took over, Toa Payoh Mall is honestly a pretty fun little time capsule to walk through. It’s laid out more like a long sheltered walkway, with rows of small shops lining both sides, similar to the kind you’d find under older apartment blocks. The whole place feels refreshingly down-to-earth and very local, which gives it a charm that’s hard to find in newer malls.

There are roughly 50 shops packed into this stretch, and they sell a surprisingly wide range of things. You’ll find bags, clothes, electronic accessories, little gadget shops, salons, massage centres, and even a few hawker-style food stalls tucked in between. It’s the kind of place where you can wander without a strict shopping list and still walk out with a couple of random, useful finds.

One of the biggest highlights here is the pricing. Most items are noticeably cheaper than what you’d pay in more modern malls, though many of the brands are lesser-known or unbranded. Still, if you’re not too picky about labels and just want good deals and a nostalgic shopping experience, this place really delivers.

Overall, Toa Payoh Mall is a charming throwback that offers affordable finds, local character, and a refreshing break from Singapore’s usual mall scene.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Open and airy community club that has space to hold multiple events. a good location for residents to bond, or to check in on community initiatives and learn about public policy.

Attended and volunteered at a community event recently there, and staff were very helpful and assisted the event organizers every step of the way.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Great place to go for classes and Interest Group. Made many friends there (Chinese, Malays, Indians). Better understanding of different cultures too!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Toa Payoh West Community Centre is the place I went for Covid vaccination.

The place is well organised, safe and efficient. It's also very windy today when I was there while waiting for my shot. It's clean and well maintained.

There's also a preschool, gym facilities, walk in career consultation, nkf dialysis centre, dance and music rooms, etc.

It is walking distance from Toa Payoh Mrt.

It's connected to a multi-storey carpark.

#ILoveToaPayohWestCC

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
This is the best Community Club in Toa Payoh. Interesting is the little cafe on the first floor. Also attached are a newly installed water playground, sandpits and basket ball courts.

The cafe has nice food! Coffee, tea, kaya toast and soft boiled eggs. :)

During covid, we came here for our vaccinations.

We also came here for our education awards.

Improvements can be made I believe, walkways with better directions and proper sheltered walkways.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Met an extremely helpful young man working at Tai Seng Mrt station by the name of Kai Yong. He spent a fair bit of time explaining to me how to get to my destination due to the underground works affecting the train direction at the moment. Super amazing service! I’m deeply impressed

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I want to compliment a crowd marshal by the name of Ricardo! He is a perfect example of excellent service where he rendered a great help to me just now by giving me an accurate and precise direction for me when I got lost in the station! He is very patient in his explanation and is always smiling even though i threw temple at him initially due to my flustered manner! He even helped me to carry my heavy luggage up the escalator as there isn't any lift at the exit.
Nevertheless I am very blessed to have met him and many thanks to him so that I can meet up with my family. Please help me to extend my thanks to him and I hope there are more such crowd marshals like him.
Thank you Ricardo and all at Tai Seng Mrt station!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
key amenities at taiseng station
1️⃣ lift located after exiting the gantry where it is exit A/C
2️⃣ toilet located after exiting the gantry where it is exit B
3️⃣ ATM (POSB/OCBC) located after exiting the gantry where it is exit C

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I was rushing for work and this morning encountered train delay and disruption. 3 trains in a row people were asked to alight at Tai Seng and wait for the next train. This caused delays to my journey and disappointment with the service.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Worst train station to be at during peak hours. At around 6-640pm, you can’t even get on the train, especially towards Bishan. Be prepared to wait for at least 3-4 trains during that time. People will just push their way in just to board the train.
Beware. But some hack for anyone who read this, there will be an empty train at 621pm. You can board that train to avoid the squeezing.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
HortPark has been around for more than a decade, and I’ve been coming here for many years — sometimes with family, sometimes for team-building activities, and sometimes just to enjoy a meal at one of the restaurants. It’s one of those places that quietly grows on you over time

What I love most is how versatile it is. The wide open lawns are perfect for kids to run around, the themed gardens are thoughtfully curated, and there are plenty of charming corners — from gazebos to shaded benches — where you can simply sit and relax. The greenery feels mature and well-established, giving the park a calm, lived-in character that newer parks sometimes lack

I’ve had meaningful team bonding sessions here, casual weekend strolls with family, and peaceful afternoons just soaking in the surroundings. It’s also conveniently connected to other green spaces, making it a great starting or ending point for a longer walk

The best time to visit is when the weather is kind — not too hot and not rainy. On breezy mornings or gentle late afternoons, HortPark truly shines. The combination of open lawns, beautiful landscaping, art features and dining options makes it a wonderful place to spend quality family time

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Always an enjoyable stop for a Saturday morning outing with family. Young kids were fascinated with how the terrapins climbed out from the pond to get a bit of sunbathing. Good opportunity for being close to and learning about nature.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
HortPark is a hidden gem that is perfectly connected to Kent Ridge Park and the Alexandra Arch, making it a great spot for a scenic walk.

The park is very well-equipped with a multi-purpose hall and several playgrounds that kids will love. For parents traveling with young children, there is a dedicated privacy room, which is a thoughtful and convenient touch.

If you are looking for a bite to eat, there are 2 or 3 restaurants on-site, including Canopy and Vineyard.

Things to note:
• The Best Part: They have water coolers throughout the park, and the water is always refreshingly cold—exactly what you need in the heat!
• Transport: There is a bus stop right outside the park entrance. nearest MRt is Labrador park MRT.
• Parking: On-site parking is available but is chargeable.

Highly recommend this park for families and hikers!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: One of my favourite places to go for a walk when I need some quiet. Peaceful, green, and not too crowded. The gardens are beautiful and well maintained, and there are plenty of shaded paths to stroll along. It’s a nice place to slow down, clear your mind, and just enjoy being surrounded by nature. A little hidden gem in Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This Park is both a park and a horticultural test bed centre. It has much to offer to visitors and it connected to the sort of nearby famous Henderson Waves via the Alexandra Arch Bridge and Forest Walk in that order.

place_name: Blk 75 Lorong 5 Toa Payoh Food Centre
review_text:
This 85 soup is actually from block 85, bedok there. This is homecook style soup which I believe is Cantonese style.

The ABC is very delicious, you can taste the sweet from the corn itself, not those 味精汤。

Thier chicken pig organ soup is delicious as well. It's peppery level is acceptable for both m'sia n s'pore taste

I highly recommend people come to drink soup from this stall

review_text: fruit juice and dry mee hoon kueh flavour was on point with a touch of homeliness. avocado fruit juice came in large size for $3.50 and apple yakult was $3. came around 6pm and there was barely any crowd which is good. did not manage to try the other stalls famous here as they were close but hope they will reopen soon!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Blk 75 Lorong 5 Toa Payoh Food Centre
review_text:
You'll find a variety of excellent, long-standing day and night stalls at the Toa Payoh Lorong 5 hawker centre. Be sure to try the popular fishball noodles and ban mian. Since the primary clientele is elderly that stay around the area, the prices are refreshingly reasonable. Some stalls also only accept cash.

The one key issue is its location; it's quite a walk from the main Toa Payoh MRT station, so it's much easier to visit if you drive. Ample parking is available in the carpark right next door.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Vivocity shopping mall is located at harborfront mrt station.inside vivocity there is harbourfront centre.therefore vivocity is a shopping mall with lots of food n shopping.there is even a library inside the mall.there r 2 kfc,2 mcdonalds in this mall one at vivo another at centre.jollibee,all American food, burger king also at this mall.2 foodcourt also 1 at vivo n 1 at centre.lots of food options(halal n non halal),many snack shops, flavored drinks,many clothes shop,popular bookstore, jewellery shops,fairprice Xtra,cold storage,7 eleven,cheers all on this mall.bread shops,pizza hut also at this mall.from this mall take a monorail at the top floor to go to Sentosa.from inside vivo mall head out n u could c a beautiful sea view.i have many times seen the sea view.from vivo mall can c lots of amazing views outside.i always spend lots of time in vivo when I come harbourfront.every1 should visit this place.every1 would definitely love it.💞 I travel to lots of places ... Eat lots of different foods... Love sharing my experience with everyone so m posting lots of google reviews ... Hope everyone loves it ... 😁

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A crowded megamall in Singapore. Can go there directly by MRT,  very convenient! The malls have so many shoplots, restaurants and cafes. A lot of branded shops can be found at here.  You can go Sentosa Singapore from here too. There is a park at rooftop as well, very chilling and relaxing. Now they have new year decoration right outside of the entrance, very attractive!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Really enjoyed visiting VivoCity 🛍️
The mall is HUGE and has pretty much everything you need. Lots of shopping, food options, cafes, and entertainment all in one place. It’s also super convenient since it connects directly to Sentosa, which makes it a great stop before or after exploring the island 🌴🚆
One of my favorite parts was definitely the terrace area 😍 The outdoor space was so pretty and relaxing, especially with the open view, greenery, and waterfront atmosphere. It felt really nice to just sit there for a while and enjoy the vibe, especially around sunset 🌅✨ Such a nice contrast from the busy indoor mall area.
The mall itself was clean, spacious, and easy to walk around despite how big it is. I also liked that there were plenty of seating areas to rest between shopping and exploring. Overall, VivoCity felt more than just a shopping mall, it was honestly a really enjoyable place to spend a few hours 🤍

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This mega mall that serves the residents in the west side of Singapore,  has upgraded recently with the introduction of more eateries at the basement, when one exits from the mrt station. Singapore is a foodie haven , so it is important to pump in popular brands to draw the hungry working people n tourists.  One can see that every outlet is packed during meal time. So it is very competitive for the shops to come up with promotional set menu to attract customers.  Another local pastime is shopping, so sale events are usually held at the front podium area. The mall houses two floors for those who enjoy shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
So many stores to choose from, this mall is huge!
The Christmas Market was great, it was lit nicely and there were a lot of good Christmas shopping options like baked goods, wrapping paper, Christmas signs and cards.
Everything was very clean but since its a huge mall it does get very very busy.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Toa Payoh Lorong 8 Market & Hawker Centre
review_text: Visited there during CNY period. Surprisingly that this place was decorated, with a hint of CNY, made the atmosphere great. This place was also clean and well maintained. The food stalls were quite alot. They segregated halal and non-halal stalls into 2 places, opposite to each other. Seats quantity was large. Food selection was wide too, definitely could find something you like. The price is affordable. Worth to pay visits.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Toa Payoh Lorong 8 Market & Hawker Centre
review_text: Came here to try the Teochew Porridge and Hokkien Mee, overall a good place to eat. Despite seeing multiple BBQ wings and satay, the venue doesn’t stink and you won’t smell like barbecued pork after leaving. Was having chicken rice cravings but didn’t spot any though.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Toa Payoh Lorong 8 Market & Hawker Centre
review_text:
Highly recommend Hong Seng Lor Mee at #01-54 that is being run by a lovely couple. The Lor Mee is one of the best I had and I wished that I had found out about this stall sooner. Love the fact that they allow us to customize the condiments (garlic, vinegar, chili, pepper) by ourselves so that we are able to enjoy it in our own ways.

Just as I was about to leave, overheard that the owners telling customers behind that the laksa is sold out. Seems like their Laksa is also very popular, would love to have a try next time!

review_text:
27 April 2026

Many nice yummy stalls with good reviews in this Hawker Centre.
Cleanliness csn be improved, washing basins surfaces seem like hasn't been cleaned for quite awhile

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Toa Payoh Lorong 8 Market & Hawker Centre
review_text:
AUG25, Lorong can be reached easily from CTE or PIE. There are plenty of hawker food, every stall has their unique taste, I like coming here for the white bee Hoon, Hokkien noodle ever the char kuay tiu is very good and the mee Hoon kuay very traditional. There is a Singapore pool but so far no luck here, after a meal not much to walk around as it is a small area surrounding the market. Of course it is always crowded ever on Monday. Highly recommend.
Market open, today 28/11 went to try the beef soup. Very good.
8/12/26, today try the mutton soup with rice @ $8.50, fantastic good. - *****.
22/11/25- 2nd time trying the mixed beef soup with rice @ 6.50. Very good soup is hot the chilli is good too. The lady very friendly and the Q was tasty ***** highly recommended.
17/1 beef kway tee. Very good by lady.
2/2 mixed beef with rice 6.50, very good.
6/2 beef soup again.
15/4 beef soup.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
My absolute go to mall for all-things sports and the typical outlet stores! Especially Nike, Puma, Adidas and Asics.

Changi City Point is one of those underrated mall spots that quietly delivers, especially if you are into sportswear and sneaker hunting.

Prices can be pretty attractive if you catch the right promos, especially for past season items. The Nike store usually has the widest selection and best deals, while Asics is great if you are looking for proper running shoes. Fila and Puma have some nice lifestyle pieces if you want something more casual and trendy.

Mall itself is spacious and not too crowded, which makes the whole shopping experience way more chill compared to town. Good spot to spend a couple of hours without feeling overwhelmed. There are also alot of good food options to be honest and also cafes to just sit down to chill after shopping for too long.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Very good shopping mall with lots of cafe and restaurant selection. All at affordable price. It's also filled with quality factory outlet for sporting products. Bought 1 crocs 2 nike 2 adidas and 2 sketcher shoes in one day with factory pricing discounts. What a steal.
Only issue is toilets need improve.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I would like to commend the mall management for their prompt response to customer feedback.

I shared feedback regarding the temperature in the mall 30 mins ago and I was pleasantly surprised that within a short period of time, the air conditioning was noticeably improved. I could already feel the mall becoming much cooler and more comfortable.

It is very encouraging to see a management team that genuinely listens to shoppers and takes action. This shows a strong commitment to providing a pleasant shopping experience for everyone.

Thank you to the management for being responsive and attentive to customer feedback. Great job and keep up the excellent work!

Recently, the mall has been noticeably warm and uncomfortable, especially during peak hours. The air conditioning does not seem sufficient, making the shopping experience less enjoyable for both shoppers and tenants.

What is particularly concerning is that the warmer environment appears to have attracted flies inside the mall, which is not something visitors would expect in a shopping centre. This affects the overall cleanliness, comfort, and customer experience.

I hope the management can look into this issue and consider lowering the air conditioning temperature or improving the cooling system. A cooler and more comfortable environment would greatly enhance the experience for shoppers and help maintain the mall’s standards.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A Unique Mall

On the outside This looks like another run of the mill mall. However, given its positioning - the mall serving the Changi “CBD” and residents living there but at the same time this isn’t exactly down town - you get a unique mix of high end stuff that is highly discounted. Some famous brands have their factory outlet in the mall.

I’ve always enjoyed coming to this mall because there are things that I would actually buy here because many things are discounted and definitely worth the buy.

This place also has numerous restaurants and coffee places when you need to take a break from the shopping.

Overall, a mall that has evolved excellently to serve the needs and wants of customers who go to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Changi City Point

5 Changi Business Park Central
1, Singapore 486038

I would like to provide some information about the CCP. If you're in the vicinity of CCP, the nearest MRT station is Expo. CCP is a shopping mall that offers a variety of shopping and dining options.

You will find numerous eateries and restaurants, as well as a grocery store, particularly NTUC FairPrice Xtra, which is excellent for purchasing daily necessities. The prices are reasonable, making it a great place to stock up on bulk items if you're driving.

For tourists visiting Singapore, there is a large hotel and service apartment called Dorset. Guests can conveniently check in here, as it is easily accessible from Changi Airport. The nearest MRT station is Tanah Merah, and tourists will need to take one more stop to reach Expo MRT Station.

With the train station just a few minutes from the hotel, guests can quickly travel to the airport and explore various attractions across Singapore, such as Bugis and Orchard, following their check-in.

Best regards,
Chris
17 Feb 2026

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. The taste was perfect, the portion was big. But wait for my local coffee for a long time. This really needs to improve

review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
AMK Hub is one of the most convenient and practical malls in Ang Mo Kio. The location is excellent, as it is directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT station and the bus interchange, making it very easy to reach by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, dining options, supermarkets, daily essentials, and services. It is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents living nearby. Whether you are looking for groceries, a quick meal, shopping, banking services, or just a place to walk around, AMK Hub has almost everything you need in one location.

I like that the mall is very accessible and easy to navigate. There are many food choices, from fast food and takeaway options to cafés and restaurants for proper meals. It is also a convenient place to run errands, meet friends, or spend time with family.

Although the mall can get quite crowded during weekends and meal times, the overall environment is still lively, clean, and well maintained. The air-conditioning is comfortable, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it easy to move around.

Overall, AMK Hub is a reliable and convenient neighbourhood mall with a good variety of food, shopping, and essential services. It is definitely one of the best places to visit in Ang Mo Kio for everyday needs, meals, and casual shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: AMK Hub is a really convenient neighbourhood mall, especially since it’s directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT and the bus interchange. It’s super easy to get to, which makes it a popular stop for quick meals or shopping. There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A good, classic neighbourhood mall with food options that leave you spoilt for choice. A rectangular-shaped mall with basements mostly eateries, Level 1 & 2 mostly shops, and Level 3 is mostly educational centres.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Went to AMK Hub for lunch. ate at that Malaysia Boleh. There are many varieties of food here suitable for your lunch or dinner...My hubby always eat the cray pot rice which comes with chicken and salted fish. That day we ate that fish ball black noodles. This store add some tasty dark sauce to their noodles which gives a different taste to it's noodles. Taste very yummy. I had their fried tang hoon. Same store...They have varies type of fried noodles which you must tried. Not bad too. We always end our meal with a bowl of Chendol which is classic with all Malaysia Boleh outlets...They definitely have this menu in all their outlets without fail...AMK Hub is filled with varieties type of food on their 3 floors esp basement 1 and basement 2...a full line of snacks and food for you to choose from. Try coming here for your meals...no regret....I have had enclosed photos of our meals that day...Enjoy!!!!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
To Principal Eunice,

I would like to express my sincere appreciation for Maple Bear Harper Junior Childcare Centre, especially under the leadership of Principal Eunice.

From the moment we enrolled our child, we have been consistently impressed by the warm, nurturing, and well-structured environment provided by the centre. The teachers are dedicated, attentive, and genuinely care about each child’s development, both academically and emotionally.

Principal Eunice has been exceptional in her leadership—always approachable, professional, and proactive in ensuring high standards across the centre. Her commitment to creating a safe, engaging, and enriching learning environment truly sets the school apart.

We have also noticed significant positive growth in our child’s confidence, communication skills, and overall enthusiasm for learning. The Centre's curriculum is thoughtfully delivered, and the activities are both fun and educational.

Thank you to Principal Eunice and the entire team for their hard work and dedication. We highly recommend Maple Bear Harper Junior to any parent looking for a reliable and quality childcare centre.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This preschool has a beautiful setup with bright classrooms and a well-designed indoor play area. The environment feels warm and welcoming, and the teachers were approachable and passionate when introducing the school. Definitely worth considering for parents exploring preschool options.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: We are incredibly thankful to the team at MapleBear Harper Junior for transitioning our daughter so seamlessly into attending school. Since starting playgroup, she has been able to articulate herself so much better and has developed into a confident and independent child. MapleBear has really helped her to develop a positive learning disposition and keenness to trying new things. The teachers are prompt and thoughtful when partnering with parents and we are assured that our daughter is in good hands!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My grandson has been under the care of lovely and dedicated teachers at MapleBear Harper Jr since he was 4 months old. The teachers are very caring and attentive. As a mother myself, I know it is not easy as full attention is needed to care for an infant. Time flies, now he is already 22 months old. He is ever so happy to be in school. He started to sing a lot and is learning how to socialise and play. He eats well, sleeps well and always looks forward to school. The teachers dutifully provide updates and feedback to parents, ensuring they are well informed of their children’s growth and progression. I would highly recommend that working parents entrust their children to their care.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Maple bear childcare experience for my grandchild since 18-month-old has been a such a happy milestone for her.  She has grown to enjoy school and its enrichment activities from the photographs taken.  From cooking with the chef’s outfit and swim and dinosaur play.
The teachers are patient and kind and give every child the needed attention.  Even at send-offs at the end of any  given day, their gesture speaks of patience.  I will definitely recommend the school to any parent who is looking for a good childcare for their precious little one.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Used to live at Woodleigh back in 2010, when the trains still skipped this station. My family used to go to Potong Pasir to catch the MRT obviously.
Coming back in 2024 it’s a very different experience. Once a deserted (and some say spooky) area, the place has become lively and filled with people. Woodleigh MRT even has a mall next door!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Quiet a nice place to hang out. Went to Starbucks to have a drink with my bbf. Order 2 hot latte grenda . Happened my bbf putting too much sugar in her latte. I went to the counter to ask for the staff to pour in hot water for the latte. Instead doing so. Carrie ( staff name) take her initiative to add hot milk to the latte and change a new mug for my friend. I was surprised that Starbucks had such a good n initiative staff going through the extra mile to do that for me. Carries! Thank you for your kind thoughts. And I will definitely come back here again ( Woodleigh mall). Kudos to you .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: MindChamps MindSpace @ Toa Payoh Central | After School Student Care for P1-P5 | MindChamps Enrichment for P1-P5
review_text:
We are extremely disappointed and frustrated with our experience at Mindspace TPY.

Firstly, the way basic needs are handled is unacceptable. The children’s water intake appears to be deliberately controlled to reduce toilet trips, and they are asked to wait until someone is available to bring them to the toilet, instead of being allowed to go when needed. This is not only unreasonable but inhumane for young children.

Safety is also a serious concern. The toilet is located outside the centre, yet supervision practices are questionable. We have personally observed a male trainer bringing a young female student to the toilet, which is highly concerning. In other instances, a female trainer simply waits outside instead of accompanying the children and ensuring the toilet environment is safe. For young kids, this is completely inadequate — there is no assurance of safety, and any parent would be worried about potential risks.

The teaching environment is equally disappointing. Staff have been heard shouting at children simply for being excited and eager to answer questions. This is not discipline — it is discouraging and creates a negative learning atmosphere.

There is also a lack of basic respect for parents’ time. Despite informing the centre in advance of a 5:30pm pick-up, children are still held back for homework or spelling during dismissal time, leaving caregivers waiting outside unnecessarily.

Communication is poor and unprofessional. Feedback from parents is acknowledged but never followed up on, with no updates or resolution provided.

The centre also fails to deliver on what was promised. We were told they would teach the “art of learning,” something they claim to be known for, yet there has been no evidence of this being implemented throughout Term 1.

On top of that, despite paying for learning materials, we are still asked to provide our own practice books for class use. We were also told that additional worksheets would be given, but we have neither seen them nor has our child received any. This raises serious questions about transparency.

Given the high monthly fees we are paying, the level of service, professionalism, and quality provided is nowhere near acceptable. It simply does not justify the cost.

Overall, this has been a very disappointing experience, and the centre needs to seriously review its practices, safety standards, and accountability.

place_name: MindChamps MindSpace @ Toa Payoh Central | After School Student Care for P1-P5 | MindChamps Enrichment for P1-P5
review_text: We had a very insightful and structured orientation. This gave us confidence in how our child will be supported when school starts. We greatly appreciate all the preparation work by the centre!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: MindChamps MindSpace @ Toa Payoh Central | After School Student Care for P1-P5 | MindChamps Enrichment for P1-P5
review_text:
I recently attended the childcare orientation and found it extremely informative, well-structured, and reassuring. The staff introduced the center’s philosophy, daily routines, safety procedures, and communication practices in a clear and organized way.

Overall, the orientation gave me confidence that my child will be in a nurturing, safe, and engaging environment. The staff came across as warm, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in the children’s development. I left feeling comfortable and well-informed about the program.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Was here for an Edusave award ceremony at the MPH, which is clean and has aircon. The open air area is also sheltered and has a huge fan so it's not hot. There are also counters to perform administrative stuff like collection of vouchers etc. There's also a Starbucks here if you wanna chill. There's a 24/7 fitness here too.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
First time booking a pickleball court for play on Tuesday (20 Jan, 8-9am) and when we arrived at the CC, the only hall that we saw was dark and empty. As the counter was not open, I called the main PA hotline and requested for assistance. The staff tried contacting someone from the CC to no avail. He said he would escalate the matter. When I asked if we should just “cancel” our court instead of continuing to wait, he told me to wait for his update. We left eventually and there was no further callback at all.

We returned to the CC at 1pm and checked with the counter staff, only to find out that the pickleball courts are not located within the CC itself, but at the HDB block opposite, on the 2nd floor. When I informed the staff that there’s no notice indicating that the courts are at a different location, the staff replied that their badminton players all know that the courts are opposite. He even asked if I’m staying in the area.

From booking, to the email acknowledgement, there’s no indication of any information stating that. Even at the CC itself, there are no notices giving directions to the courts. Another staff then came out and added that the information on the poster did not say that the courts are in the hall within the CC itself and that the opposite block is part of Bishan CC.

Totally appalled at the attitude of the counter staff, who seemed to imply that it’s our fault for not knowing where the courts are.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The volunteer staff there were very polite and provided clear, correct guidance. I’m very grateful for their service—it solved my problem and helped me save a considerable amount of money.
Ann Tan

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: It’s a cool place to hangout or wait for a friend to meetup. It’s covered so no issues during rain. There is free water refilling, toilet, and Starbucks too if you need coffee. There’s a lot of exercise activities in the morning for seniors, some activities during the day, and cheering squads use it for practice in the evenings. It’s a bustling place and I am glad to be living nearby and can go there anytime.

review_text: To the bus 238 driver at 1.39pm on 15 Feb. You saw me and stopped for 1 sec before driving off..leaving me stranded outside the interchange. I tried all the doors and wasn't able to return inside. So heartless and cruel of you.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Cleanliness of the bus interchange was good. Bus interchange is near to alot of eateries and HDB hub. Good location and very convenient.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Standard bus interchange, however air-conditioned. Well-connected to the HDB Hub which offers plenty of stores. Directions can be a bi confusing for first-timers to this interchange but this interchange is well connected to many parts of Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Not the first time I had to wait for a bus for more than 30 minutes when it showed about 15 minutes waiting time on the tv display. Please ensure the bus drivers are well aware that they are supposed to be reaching at their designated timing and not make passengers who are going to work or school wait this long. The do not have anything to rush for since they are already at work driving buses but we are rushing for time so that we can be on time for work/school.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The food court located beneath the bus interchange provides a multitude of reasonably priced food options. You can find a diverse range of local delicacies and Asian favorites to choose from.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: The mutation orchid garden is truly stunning and unique, filled with rare and vibrant blooms that capture attention at every corner. Each orchid displays unusual colors, intricate patterns, and elegant shapes, creating a magical atmosphere for visitors. The garden is carefully arranged, allowing guests to walk through and admire the beauty up close. However, the strong sunlight makes the visit slightly tiring, especially during midday. Despite the heat, the breathtaking scenery and peaceful environment make the experience worthwhile. With a bit more shade or a cooler time of day, the visit would be even more enjoyable and relaxing for everyone.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Been here many times.  Still think it is a good place to relax.
The garden is very well planned and full of greenery.
There is also a new indoor garden air-conditioning space where you can enjoy completely different species of orchids.
If it rains, there are many places to hide.  There are also drinks vending machines.
Even if you need to pay in the park, I suggest you come.  It's worth it!👍

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Absolutely spectacular! These garden were Beautiful and very well put together. Clearly a lot of thought has been put into the displays. They also had spots specifically set up for photo opportunities which helps to reduce clogging on the paths. There are bathrooms and places to get refreshments near by. There are also shaded areas throughout the gardens where you can shelter from the rain or sun as needed.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well manicured and maintained, this place is. The design of this part of Botanic gardens takes you on a journey through meandering pathways and cooling glasshouse. Certainly worth the entrance fee. Orchids are one of the most intriguing flowers. As the old adage goes necessity is the mother of evolution, these orchids are perfectly evolved to benefit from their symbiotic pollinators. Some uses trickery to entice the male species of insects to land on the stigma, some make the landing pad comfortable for a nice sip of delicious nectar. Amazing!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Absolutely stunning the highlight of our trip for me. An incredible number of beautiful orchids in a lovely landscaped and meandering garden. Entry is just 5 dollars making this easily one of the most affordable things to do in Singapore, and absolutely worthwhile. You could easily spend a day wandering the orchid garden and the surrounding botanic gardens

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This hawker center was the first place I visited right after I landed in Singapore. Personally, I cannot say that this was the best in Singapore since I have not yet tried other places at the time.

As a first time traveler, Newton Hawkers were so welcoming! I had a great time with the vibes by the uncles and aunties offering their products to me. Majority of the crowd are also local, which is a plus for me. This specific stall (in the picture) was the best (service-wise).

In the photo, I have made friends with Auntie Nico and Uncle John. I don’t know their relationship or if they own the store but they treated me so well and even offered to show me how they cook their food (I told them that I own a restaurant and was curious about how they cook their food).

In the end of the night, I asked them to take a picture with me and they did. Auntie Nico also used her phone to get a selfie with me lol. Fun times!

There’s always something beautiful about hard working people and everytime you get a chance to talk to them, do it!

WILL ALWAYS VISIT HERE EVERYTIME I GO TO SINGAPORE!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Newton Food Centre is a must-visit in Singapore. Lively, authentic, and packed with incredible food options, it’s everything you want from a classic hawker centre.

The variety is outstanding — from local favourites to late-night comfort dishes, there’s something for everyone. Prices are very reasonable, portions are generous, and the flavours are bold and authentic. It’s the kind of place where you can try multiple dishes in one visit and still want to come back again.

The atmosphere is busy and energetic, especially in the evenings, but that’s part of the charm. Despite the crowds, there’s plenty of seating and the space is well laid out. Being open 24 hours is a huge bonus, whether you’re after a late supper, early breakfast, or anything in between.

Great spot for travellers wanting a genuine local experience, groups with different tastes, or anyone keen to eat well without breaking the bank. Casual, vibrant, and full of character — exactly what a Singapore hawker centre should be.

🍜 Huge variety of food
🌶️ Authentic local flavours
💰 Affordable prices
⏰ Open 24 hours
🎉 Lively atmosphere

Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
• Food
The food selection at this center is a reliable choice for local flavors. The chicken wings and satay are particular highlights, especially when paired with a cold beverage. Another notable mention is the Gong Gong, which is a dish that often prompts a visit to this location. The flavors are authentic and provide a satisfying taste of local seafood and grilled items.

• Service & Atmosphere
The atmosphere is lively and typical of a popular open-air food center in Singapore. It offers a vibrant setting that is suitable for casual gatherings. While the environment can get busy and crowded, it remains a practical and energetic spot for a late-night meal or a social drink. The service varies by stall but is generally efficient and functional.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Well known hawker centre that once upon a time catered mostly to tourists.

But locals are now crowding the place because of the good food and the variety and because the Singapore Tourism Board has successfully clamped down some seafood stalls touting and scamming patrons, especially tourists, with exorbitant prices.

This is one hawker centre where you will find people from all over the world savouring local street food of all races in Singapore which originated from China, India,  Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand,  etc and even the US.

Beer by the jugs are served here.

Most stalls are closed for lunch. But it is usually quite difficult to get a table during dinner time, especially on weekends. Sharing table is the norm.  So be willing to do so.

Queuing at some stalls to collect your own order is also the norm.

This hawker centre, including the free toilets, are very clean and airy. There are basins all over for patrons to wash their hands before and after meals
meals.

Pay when you order. In cash or via your phone transfer.

There is an adjoining carpark. A huge one. But it can still be hard to find a parking space during dinner time.

The Newton MRT is very near by.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A true foodie paradise, with flavors from across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and China all coming together in one place. You’ll find yourself eating alongside both locals and tourists, which adds to the energy and authenticity. Not too crowded on a Monday night, making it easy to explore. Highlights included the fresh sugar cane juice, black pepper crab, Singapore chili stingray, fried squid, and the classic Singapore “carrot cake.” A great way to experience the diversity of the region’s street food culture.

This is one hawker centre where you will find people from all over the world savouring local street food of all races in Singapore which originated from China, India,  Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand,  etc and even the US.

Beer by the jugs are served here.

Most stalls are closed for lunch. But it is usually quite difficult to get a table during dinner time, especially on weekends. Sharing table is the norm.  So be willing to do so.

Queuing at some stalls to collect your own order is also the norm.

This hawker centre, including the free toilets, are very clean and airy. There are basins all over for patrons to wash their hands before and after meals
meals.

Pay when you order. In cash or via your phone transfer.

There is an adjoining carpark. A huge one. But it can still be hard to find a parking space during dinner time.

The Newton MRT is very near by.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This hawker center was the first place I visited right after I landed in Singapore. Personally, I cannot say that this was the best in Singapore since I have not yet tried other places at the time.

As a first time traveler, Newton Hawkers were so welcoming! I had a great time with the vibes by the uncles and aunties offering their products to me. Majority of the crowd are also local, which is a plus for me. This specific stall (in the picture) was the best (service-wise).

In the photo, I have made friends with Auntie Nico and Uncle John. I don’t know their relationship or if they own the store but they treated me so well and even offered to show me how they cook their food (I told them that I own a restaurant and was curious about how they cook their food).

In the end of the night, I asked them to take a picture with me and they did. Auntie Nico also used her phone to get a selfie with me lol. Fun times!

There’s always something beautiful about hard working people and everytime you get a chance to talk to them, do it!

WILL ALWAYS VISIT HERE EVERYTIME I GO TO SINGAPORE!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

My elderly sister (75yo) stays at block 417.  She just gotton her new mobile phone.  She is not a tech person, should say sometimes quite fearful (as nowadays too many scam cases via mobile usage).  She was finally encouraged  to made an appointment to "Learn Digital @Teck Ghee CC" on 2nd May at 2 pm.
She was glad the Digital ambassador assigned to her Ms Sow Fong was effective, patient and friendly.  The 45mins session managed to enlighten her of some important issues and my sister was very comfortable with her.  She wanted to get another appointment with Ms Sow Fong, but was told that she does not have a fixed schedule time with the cc.
Of course, should Ms Sow Fong be scheduled to TeckGhee cc again, my sister will surely want to learn more from her.
Nonetheless, my sister reminded me to pen a review for Ms Sow Fong.
Thump Up for Ms Sow Fong.
